32.reason: [The format string is incorrect. It should be "%d " and the expression s[top--] should be outside the quotes as an argument to printf. As written, it prints the literal string ",s[top--]" instead of the popped value.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The format string is incorrect. It should be "%d " and the expression s[top--] should be outside the quotes as an argument to printf. As written, it prints the literal string ",s[top--]" instead of the popped value.]

40.reason: [Using " %d " with a trailing space in scanf can cause unexpected blocking behavior because it waits for non-whitespace input after the integer. This may lead to issues in reading the next operation correctly, especially at the end of input.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using " %d " with a trailing space in scanf can cause unexpected blocking behavior because it waits for non-whitespace input after the integer. This may lead to issues in reading the next operation correctly, especially at the end of input.]

43.reason: [Same issue as above: trailing space in format string may cause scanf to wait for additional non-whitespace input unnecessarily.]
